title: Behind the scenes — the ingest
surface: scripts/ingest-album.ts — one command, directly to the DB, no EXIF round-trip
summary: What makes a new event findable-by-jersey the moment it lands.

strategy.decision: Every new album flows through **one command** (`ingest-album.ts`): upload → extract → embed → write `photo_metadata` + `photo_jersey_sightings`, directly to the DB. That is what makes a new event findable-by-jersey the moment it lands.
strategy.why: The find step is only as good as the sightings behind it (`prescription.yml`). The #10 ingest writes sightings on every photo; the legacy enrich → sync → EXIF chain that used to (mis)feed it was **removed** in the cutover (`decisions/0004`).
strategy.shipped: `scripts/ingest-album.ts` — reprocess-in-place, idempotent, album-authoritative sport (the enforce_album_sport trigger), a single structured prompt (`extractOne`), OpenRouter vision + embeddings. Proven on BPO 2026 (119 photos, zero failures).
strategy.gap: Existing albums were built by the legacy path (extraction_version=0); only new albums carry #10 provenance — the clean-cutover choice (A): new-only, no back-stamp re-ingest.
strategy.question: Back-stamp the legacy albums for `extraction_version` provenance, or leave them (provenance only buys future surgical re-runs)?

currentState.route: scripts/ingest-album.ts (operator CLI, not a user surface)
currentState.summary: The live new-album path: one resumable module, upload + extract + embed + DB write + sightings per image.
currentState.annotation: The legacy enrich-local-photos.ts / sync-local-to-supabase.ts / run-pipeline.ts chain + the players[] write were deleted in the #10 cutover. `players` column DROP is the last (operator) step.
